title: symphyotrichum dumosum
intro: Symphyotrichum dumosum, also known as New England aster or white wood aster, is a flowering plant species native to eastern North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which offer health benefits like reducing fever and alleviating coughs. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antiviral and antimicrobial properties, demonstrating its potential in modern medicine.
